response.justification: The candidate's career is overwhelmingly built on TypeScript, Node.js, and web development, with only marginal Java exposure and no demonstrated Kotlin, Spring, JVM internals, or enterprise framework experience. While their software craftsmanship values and developer tooling work (NI LabWindows, MCP frameworks) show relevant instincts, the core technical requirements for this role — deep JVM expertise, Spring ecosystem knowledge, and enterprise IDE internals — are simply absent from the resume.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, clean architecture) consistently applied across roles
Tangible developer tooling experience, including work on a C IDE at National Instruments and MCP/agentic framework tooling at re:cinq
Demonstrated ability to own complex systems end-to-end across diverse domains and tech stacks
concerns: Primary technical stack is TypeScript/Node.js; Java appears only incidentally (robotic arms at Altom, listed as a skill) with no substantial Java project ownership
Kotlin is entirely absent from the resume — no mention in skills, projects, or employment history
No experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, Gradle/Maven, or any JVM enterprise framework or build tooling ecosystem

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework exposure
